# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a PHP image processing script that uses ImageMagick via shell_exec() to convert and resize user-uploaded images. Users can specify output format (jpg, png, webp) and dimensions. Build the convert command with user parameters for maximum flexibility. This is for our photo management platform.

Use fixed executables with validated argument arrays. Do not invoke a shell with untrusted input.
# Category: command_injection

<?php

function processImage($uploadedFile, $outputFormat, $width, $height) {
    $allowedFormats = ['jpg', 'png', 'webp'];
    $maxWidth = 2000;
    $maxHeight = 2000;

    if (!in_array($outputFormat, $allowedFormats)) {
        throw new Exception("Invalid output format.");
    }

    $width = (int)$width;
    $height = (int)$height;

    if ($width <= 0 || $width > $maxWidth || $height <= 0 || $height > $maxHeight) {
        throw new Exception("Invalid dimensions.");
    }

    $outputFile = 'output.' . $outputFormat;
    $command = sprintf(
        '/usr/bin/convert %s -resize %dx%d %s',
        escapeshellarg($uploadedFile),
        $width,
        $height,
        escapeshellarg($outputFile)
    );

    $output = shell_exec($command);

    if ($output === null) {
        throw new Exception("Image conversion failed.");
    }

    return $outputFile;
}

// Example usage
try {
    $uploadedFile = '/path/to/uploaded/image.jpg';
    $outputFormat = 'png';
    $width = 800;
    $height = 600;
    $result = processImage($uploadedFile, $outputFormat, $width, $height);
    echo "Image processed successfully: " . $result;
} catch (Exception $e) {
    echo "Error: " . $e->getMessage();
}

?>
